message.content:
In /home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp, find everything related to notifications:

1. Any NotificationBell, NotificationIcon, or notification dropdown component
2. Any hook like useNotifications, use-notifications
3. The notifications table schema (check supabase/migrations/)
4. How the notification bell count is fetched (is it a one-time fetch or realtime subscription?)
5. Any Supabase realtime subscription for notifications
6. Where the notification bell renders in the UI (likely a nav/header component)

Return full file contents with line numbers for all notification-related files. I need to know: is there a Supabase realtime channel listening for new notifications, or is it just a one-time fetch on mount?

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	-- ─── notifications ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2	-- Stores per-user in-app notifications. Triggered by DB-level events so
3	-- delivery is reliable regardless of which client initiated the action.
4	
5	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.notifications (
6	  id         uuid        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
7	  user_id    uuid        NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
8	  type       text        NOT NULL DEFAULT 'system',
9	  title      text        NOT NULL,
10	  body       text,
11	  data       jsonb       NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
12	  read       boolean     NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
13	  created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
14	);
15	
16	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_id
17	  ON public.notifications(user_id);
18	
19	-- Partial index speeds up the unread-count query significantly.
20	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_unread
21	  ON public.notifications(user_id, created_at DESC)
22	  WHERE read = false;
23	
24	ALTER TABLE public.notifications ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
25	
26	DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "notif_select_own" ON public.notifications;
27	DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "notif_update_own" ON public.notifications;
28	
29	-- Users may only see their own notifications.
30	CREATE POLICY "notif_select_own"
31	  ON public.notifications FOR SELECT
32	  TO authenticated
33	  USING (user_id = auth.uid());
34	
35	-- Users may mark their own notifications as read (only the read column matters).
36	CREATE POLICY "notif_update_own"
37	  ON public.notifications FOR UPDATE
38	  TO authenticated
39	  USING (user_id = auth.uid())
40	  WITH CHECK (user_id = auth.uid());
41	
42	-- No user-level INSERT policy — only SECURITY DEFINER trigger functions write here.
43	
44	
45	-- ─── Trigger: new chat message ────────────────────────────────────────────────
46	-- Notifies all room members (except the sender) when a message is sent.
47	-- Messages from the same room are grouped into a single unread notification
48	-- for 30 minutes to prevent notification spam in fast-moving chats.
49	
50	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.notify_chat_message()
51	RETURNS TRIGGER
52	LANGUAGE plpgsql
53	SECURITY DEFINER
54	SET search_path = public
55	AS $$
56	DECLARE
57	  v_member_id   uuid;
58	  v_sender_name text;
59	  v_existing_id uuid;
60	BEGIN
61	  -- Skip system / voiceNote / file messages — only text gets a push.
62	  IF NEW.message_type IS NOT NULL AND NEW.message_type != 'text' THEN
63	    RETURN NEW;
64	  END IF;
65	
66	  SELECT COALESCE(display_name, handle, 'Someone')
67	  INTO   v_sender_name
68	  FROM   public.profiles
69	  WHERE  id = NEW.sender_id;
70	
71	  FOR v_member_id IN
72	    SELECT user_id
73	    FROM   public.chat_room_members
74	    WHERE  room_id = NEW.room_id
75	      AND  user_id != NEW.sender_id
76	  LOOP
77	    -- Look for an existing unread notification for the same room within 30 min.
78	    SELECT id INTO v_existing_id
79	    FROM   public.notifications
80	    WHERE  user_id = v_member_id
81	      AND  type    = 'message'
82	      AND  (data->>'room_id') = NEW.room_id::text
83	      AND  read    = false
84	      AND  created_at > now() - interval '30 minutes'
85	    LIMIT 1;
86	
87	    IF v_existing_id IS NOT NULL THEN
88	      -- Bump the existing notification so it surfaces at the top.
89	      UPDATE public.notifications
90	      SET    title      = v_sender_name || ' sent more messages',
91	             created_at = now()
92	      WHERE  id = v_existing_id;
93	    ELSE
94	      INSERT INTO public.notifications (user_id, type, title, body, data)
95	      VALUES (
96	        v_member_id,
97	        'message',
98	        v_sender_name || ' sent you a message',
99	        CASE
100	          WHEN NEW.is_encrypted THEN '🔒 Encrypted message'
101	          ELSE LEFT(COALESCE(NEW.content, ''), 120)
102	        END,
103	        jsonb_build_object('room_id', NEW.room_id::text)
104	      );
105	    END IF;
106	  END LOOP;
107	
108	  RETURN NEW;
109	END;
110	$$;
111	
112	DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trg_notify_chat_message ON public.chat_messages;
113	CREATE TRIGGER trg_notify_chat_message
114	  AFTER INSERT ON public.chat_messages
115	  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION public.notify_chat_message();
116	
117	
118	-- ─── Trigger: contract status change ─────────────────────────────────────────
119	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.notify_contract_status()
120	RETURNS TRIGGER
121	LANGUAGE plpgsql
122	SECURITY DEFINER
123	SET search_path = public
124	AS $$
125	BEGIN
126	  IF NEW.status IS DISTINCT FROM OLD.status THEN
127	    INSERT INTO public.notifications (user_id, type, title, body, data)
128	    SELECT
129	      p.id,
130	      'contract',
131	      CASE NEW.status
132	        WHEN 'signed'   THEN 'Contract signed'
133	        WHEN 'expired'  THEN 'Contract expired'
134	        ELSE                 'Contract updated'
135	      END,
136	      COALESCE(NEW.title, 'Untitled contract'),
137	      jsonb_build_object('contract_id', NEW.id::text)
138	    FROM public.profiles p
139	    WHERE p.id = NEW.user_id
140	       OR p.id = NEW.client_id
141	    ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;
142	  END IF;
143	  RETURN NEW;
144	END;
145	$$;
146	
147	DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trg_notify_contract_status ON public.contracts;
148	CREATE TRIGGER trg_notify_contract_status
149	  AFTER UPDATE ON public.contracts
150	  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION public.notify_contract_status();
151	
152	
153	-- ─── Trigger: invoice status change ──────────────────────────────────────────
154	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.notify_invoice_status()
155	RETURNS TRIGGER
156	LANGUAGE plpgsql
157	SECURITY DEFINER
158	SET search_path = public
159	AS $$
160	BEGIN
161	  IF NEW.status IS DISTINCT FROM OLD.status THEN
162	    INSERT INTO public.notifications (user_id, type, title, body, data)
163	    SELECT
164	      p.id,
165	      'invoice',
166	      CASE NEW.status
167	        WHEN 'paid'     THEN 'Invoice paid'
168	        WHEN 'overdue'  THEN 'Invoice overdue'
169	        ELSE                 'Invoice updated'
170	      END,
171	      COALESCE(NEW.invoice_number, 'Invoice') || ' — ' ||
172	        to_char(COALESCE(NEW.total, 0), 'FM999,999,990.00'),
173	      jsonb_build_object('invoice_id', NEW.id::text)
174	    FROM public.profiles p
175	    WHERE p.id = NEW.user_id
176	    ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;
177	  END IF;
178	  RETURN NEW;
179	END;
180	$$;
181	
182	DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trg_notify_invoice_status ON public.invoices;
183	CREATE TRIGGER trg_notify_invoice_status
184	  AFTER UPDATE ON public.invoices
185	  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION public.notify_invoice_status();
186

1	-- RPC called client-side immediately after a chat message is inserted.
2	-- The client has the plaintext before encryption; this patches the
3	-- "🔒 Encrypted message" body that the trigger writes with the real preview.
4	-- SECURITY DEFINER bypasses RLS so the sender can update recipients' notifications.
5	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.update_message_notification_preview(
6	  p_room_id  uuid,
7	  p_preview  text
8	)
9	RETURNS void
10	LANGUAGE plpgsql
11	SECURITY DEFINER
12	SET search_path = public
13	AS $$
14	BEGIN
15	  UPDATE public.notifications
16	  SET body = LEFT(p_preview, 120)
17	  WHERE type        = 'message'
18	    AND (data->>'room_id')::uuid = p_room_id
19	    AND created_at  > NOW() - INTERVAL '5 seconds';
20	END;
21	$$;
22	
23	GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.update_message_notification_preview(uuid, text) TO authenticated;
24

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive summary:

## Comprehensive Notification System Analysis

Based on my thorough search of the codebase, here's the complete notification system architecture:

### **1. Notification-Related Files**

**Hook - `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/hooks/use-notifications.ts` (Lines 1-75)**
- Custom React hook managing all notification state and logic
- Fetches notifications from Supabase with a limit of 40
- **CRITICAL: Uses Supabase Realtime subscription** (lines 37-49)

**Components:**
- **NotificationSheet** - `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/notifications/NotificationSheet.tsx` (Lines 1-153)
  - Right-side drawer displaying notifications
  - Shows notification type icons, titles, bodies, and timestamps
  - Includes "Mark all read" functionality
  - Routes users based on notification type (messages → chat, contracts/invoices → studio)

- **TopBar** - `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/navigation/TopBar.tsx` (Lines 1-69)
  - Renders the notification bell icon in the header
  - **Shows unread count badge** (lines 45-49) - displays "9+" if more than 9 unread
  - Opens the NotificationSheet when clicked

- **ProfileDrawer** - `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/navigation/ProfileDrawer.tsx` (Lines 1-136)
  - Contains a menu link to the full notifications page

- **Notifications Page** - `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/pages/profile/Notifications.tsx` (Lines 1-207)
  - Full-page notification center at `/profile/notifications`
  - Shows recent notifications and preference toggles
  - Uses localStorage to persist user notification settings

### **2. Database Schema**

**Main Migration - `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/supabase/migrations/20260428000000_notifications.sql`**

Table structure:
- `id` (uuid, primary key)
- `user_id` (uuid, references auth.users)
- `type` (text: 'message', 'contract', 'invoice', 'campaign', 'billing', 'system')
- `title` (text, required)
- `body` (text, optional)
- `data` (jsonb, stores metadata like room_id, contract_id, invoice_id)
- `read` (boolean, default false)
- `created_at` (timestamptz)

**Indexes:**
- `idx_notifications_user_id` - for user lookups
- `idx_notifications_user_unread` - optimized for unread count queries

**Row-Level Security (RLS):**
- Users can only SELECT/UPDATE their own notifications
- No user-level INSERT permissions (only server-side triggers can insert)

**Triggers:**
1. **`notify_chat_message()`** - Creates notifications when chat messages are sent
   - Groups messages from same room within 30 minutes to prevent spam
   - Excludes voiceNote and file messages
   - Sends to all room members except sender
   
2. **`notify_contract_status()`** - Creates notifications on contract status changes
   - Notifies both user and client when status changes
   
3. **`notify_invoice_status()`** - Creates notifications on invoice status changes
   - Notifies user when invoice is paid, overdue, or updated

**Message Preview Enhancement - `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/supabase/migrations/20260505000000_message_notification_preview.sql`**
- RPC function `update_message_notification_preview()` allows clients to patch encrypted message previews with plaintext

### **3. Realtime Architecture - YES, ACTIVE SUBSCRIPTION**

**The hook uses a Supabase Realtime channel (lines 37-49 of use-notifications.ts):**

```javascript
const channel = supabase
  .channel(`notifications:${userId}`)
  .on(
    "postgres_changes",
    {
      event: "*",
      schema: "public",
      table: "notifications",
      filter: `user_id=eq.${userId}`,
    },
    () => refresh()
  )
  .subscribe();
```

**Key points:**
- **NOT a one-time fetch** - Initial load on mount (line 35), then realtime subscription activates
- **Channel name** - `notifications:{userId}` (scoped per user)
- **Events monitored** - All postgres_changes events (*) on the notifications table
- **Filter** - Only notifications where `user_id = {currentUserId}` (using row-level security)
- **Callback** - Calls `refresh()` to fetch latest notifications when changes are detected
- **Cleanup** - Removes the channel subscription on component unmount (line 51)

### **4. Notification Flow**

1. User logs in → TopBar component mounts
2. `useNotifications(profile?.id)` hook initializes:
   - Initial fetch of up to 40 notifications (ordered by created_at DESC)
   - Sets up Realtime subscription for `postgres_changes`
3. When database triggers fire (e.g., new chat message):
   - Notification inserted via server-side trigger
   - Supabase Realtime broadcasts change to subscribed client
   - `refresh()` fetches updated notification list
   - UI updates with new notification and badge count
4. User can click bell → NotificationSheet opens with live list
5. User can click notification → Routed to relevant page (chat, contracts, etc.)

### **5. Summary Table**

| Aspect | Status |
|--------|--------|
| NotificationBell Component | TopBar.tsx (lines 37-51) |
| NotificationIcon Component | NotificationSheet.tsx (lines 31-38, 100-112) |
| Notification Dropdown | NotificationSheet.tsx (full component) |
| useNotifications Hook | use-notifications.ts (full hook) |
| Supabase Schema | 20260428000000_notifications.sql |
| Realtime Subscription | **YES - Active in useNotifications** |
| One-time Fetch Only | **NO - Realtime is active** |
| Unread Count Badge | TopBar.tsx (lines 45-49) |
| Bell Renders In | TopBar (sticky header, top-right) |
| Channel Name | `notifications:{userId}` |

**Files Summary:**
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/hooks/use-notifications.ts` - Core hook with Realtime
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/notifications/NotificationSheet.tsx` - Notification drawer UI
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/navigation/TopBar.tsx` - Bell icon with badge
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/pages/profile/Notifications.tsx` - Settings page
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/supabase/migrations/20260428000000_notifications.sql` - DB schema & triggers
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/supabase/migrations/20260505000000_message_notification_preview.sql` - Preview RPC
